text:
WIC: Breastfeeding Support | Sutter County, CA
Show/Hide
ROAD CLOSURES
COMMUNITY ACADEMY
We know that every family’s feeding journey is unique.
WIC is here to support you however you choose to feed your baby
—whether that’s exclusive breastfeeding, combination feeding, or formula feeding. Our goal is to make sure you feel supported, confident, and cared for every step of the way.
WIC supports the
American Academy of Pediatrics’ recommendation
for exclusive breastfeeding for about the first 6 months, with continued breastfeeding for 2 years or longer as mutually desired by the mother and baby.
At WIC, we
encourage breastfeeding
. All WIC staff are trained to promote breastfeeding and provide the guidance, reassurance, and encouragement mothers and infants need for success.
Breastfeeding Support at WIC
WIC provides support
in person, by phone, and through text
, so you can connect with us in the way that works best for you.
We offer
prenatal education
about breastfeeding and check in after your baby is born to help with feeding. Every WIC staff member is trained in breastfeeding support and ready to help you along your journey.
Peer Counseling Program
Our
WIC Peer Counselor Program
provides personalized, mom-to-mom support throughout your breastfeeding journey. Peer counselors are mothers who have breastfed their own babies and are trained to help support you with encouragement and practical advice.
Peer counselors check in regularly to provide:
Education and encouragement
“How-to” help during pregnancy and after your baby’s birth
Answers to questions and concerns
Tips and guidance to help feeding get off to a positive start
Breast Pumps
WIC can help you get set up with a breast pump that fits your needs. We can:
Assist you in exploring your
insurance pump options
Loan
hospital-grade pumps
when medically necessary
Provide
manual hand pumps
for short-term or occasional use
If you’re returning to work or school, WIC is here to help you continue breastfeeding while away from your baby—with both equipment and ongoing support.
Additional Breastfeeding Resources
Explore more information and education to help you feel confident and prepared.
Learn more about breastfeeding basics
here
.
